raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I made a one-time {$1.00} purchase on XXXX to download a PDF version of a passport form. The document provided was not the correct one, but since the charge was small, I did not dispute it. 

Without clear disclosure or my consent, the merchant enrolled me in a recurring {$49.00} monthly subscription. The subscription terms were not stated clearly at the time of purchase, and I did not knowingly authorize ongoing charges. This constitutes an unauthorized recurring charge and is a violation of my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). 

I have been charged {$49.00} on XX/XX/year>, and again on XX/XX/year>. I contacted the merchant by phone and email on XX/XX/year>, requesting a refund, but they refused and only canceled my account. 

I then contacted my card issuer, American Express, to dispute the charges. The representative stated that they would file the dispute but also said there was a 99.99 % chance it wont be investigated. This response fails to meet their legal obligations under the FCBA, which requires creditors to investigate billing errors and unauthorized charges.

I am requesting that the CFPB require American Express to : Properly investigate these charges & reverse the unauthorized billing.

I have attached screenshots of the disputed transactions for review. 

I am also an XXXX XXXX servicemember and request that this complaint be reviewed by the CFPB XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX.
